chunk_map.heading: You steer the answer by loading context
The loop is the cargo, not the fix

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 3 "One more window" / "The suggestion shifts again" is an immediate continuation of chunk 2's demo subject — compendium's own worked non-violation example. chunk 4 "the trick you named at the opening trick-exchange" resolves against getting-going.md's 'Start here' trick-naming beat, which precedes this lecture in the module's include order (module-arc-beats-in-openers/closers carve-out; this lecture is the M1 opener placed right after that beat per the module's Meta 'Opening-bid install mechanic').

chunk 3's opener ("A role colors answers the same way a fact does") is a sequential same-file callback to chunk 2's demo, not a cross-file or backward-file positional pointer — explicitly fine per the compendium ("within one file, sequential callbacks are fine").

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The trick-exchange beat (chunk 4) exists in getting-going.md's 'Start here' section, ahead of this lecture in module order.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Live-demo beats survive a text read.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 2 "Context is King": guess prompt ("Take a guess before it runs. What does Claude suggest now?") precedes its reveal ("Something Finnish: salmon, rye bread, meatballs.") in reading order in the current working-tree text.

chunk 3's "That's the move" bullet reads as a single sentence ("The move is loading the right context before the question, not a clever prompt or a slash command you didn't know") — not the banned 'Not X. Not Y. Z.' triple-sentence construction. No anaphora triples, self-grading superlatives, unattested number+retraction, abstraction-noun stacks, or empty-leg verb triplets found elsewhere.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 4 names 'the opening trick-exchange', not the maintainer-dialect 'Connections' — room-legible language.
